bit_accounting.note_overcharge: EXPERT_INDEX_BITS=7=ceil(log2 128) but only 64 survivors exist; 6 bits suffice.

shared_headroom_double_count.note: both lanes charge against the same 54,121,610-bit headroom over the same sealed 0.999769787 base and each declares itself legal independently; jointly still legal (3.9 percent) but no lane nets the other out.

bit_accounting.status: CORRECT, reproduced to the digit; conservative (over-charges the index field)

layer_selection_bias.note: the three probed layers are the bottom quintile of survivor retention; the report discloses only that they are shallow.

moe_block_output_vs_residual_L0.consequence: the verdict's '~0.30 relative perturbation injected at a layer' is ~8x overstated in residual-stream terms.
moe_block_output_vs_residual_L0.method: ||y_moe|| estimated as ||f_top1|| * sqrt(sum_k w_k^2) over the true top-8 router weights, i.e. assumes comparable per-expert output norms across the top-8. Order-of-magnitude, L0 only, same probe sentence.

not_refuted: No proxy laundering. Inputs are the real post-attention-layernorm residual from qwen_real_forward on the real 118-shard checkpoint; weights are real bf16; the claim scope is explicitly weight/activation space and no capability claim is made.
Bit accounting is exact and reproduces to the digit at both granularities; the complete artifact with the scalar merge is 0.9997721412657545 BPW, legal.
The scalar merge is genuinely affordable and genuinely does not work; the per-row merge is genuinely illegal by 1,523,105,142 bits (181.57 MiB).
The recommendation 'treat S64 as unadjudicated until a real parent-vs-packed forward with a task score' is correct and is unaffected by every refutation above.
recommended_correction: Keep the merge-is-dead half; it survives every attack and the in-sample contamination only strengthens it. Fix three things before sealing: (1) drop the word 'held out' for any omitted expert with fewer than 2 routed tokens, or exclude those experts and report the median over experts with n>=2 separately - and rebuild y_c excluding leaked singletons before quoting moe_merge_rel_err_affected_heldout; (2) restate the S64 cost in residual-stream terms (~0.036 at L0, not ~0.30) or stop quoting a per-layer magnitude at all, and disclose that L0/L3/L7 are the bottom quintile of survivor retention; (3) before declaring the merge axis closed, run one m=64 scalar merge with correlation-selected predictors on a single layer - it costs 16.4 percent of the headroom the ledger already has and is the only setting the budget leaves free. Separately, rename qwen_structural_plan.rd_floor: it is the Gaussian upper bound, not a floor.
refutations: FALSE HELD-OUT LABEL. 13/28, 16/26 and 11/27 omitted experts at L0/L3/L7 are routed to on exactly ONE probe token. `fit_hit = [p for p in hit if p%2==0] or hit` and `sc = [p for p in hit if p%2==1] or hit` both fall back to the full hit list, so for those experts the fit set and the score set are the SAME token. The headline medians (0.885/0.993/0.995 and 0.863/0.988/0.995) are in-sample for roughly half their entries and the median sits on them. Honesty caveat 6 as written is false. Direction favours the merge, so the scientific conclusion survives and is in fact strengthened.

ORACLE LEAK INTO THE BLOCK-LEVEL HELD-OUT NUMBER. A singleton omitted expert whose only hit is an odd token gets its coefficients fit on that token, and y_c is then scored on the same token. moe_merge_rel_err_affected_heldout (0.216/0.179/0.360) is therefore contaminated in the merge's favour. Same direction, same survival of the conclusion, but the number is not clean.

UNDISCLOSED ADVERSARIAL LAYER SELECTION. L0/L3/L7 sit at percentile 0.000/0.021/0.202 of the 94-layer keep-64 retention distribution (L0 is the single worst layer in the model). The report calls them only 'shallow'.

SEVERITY OVERCLAIM. 'A ~0.30 relative perturbation injected at a layer' conflates the MoE block output with the residual stream. Measured at L0 with 3 expert tensors: the block output is ~13 percent of the residual norm, so a 0.274 block-output relative error is a ~0.036 residual-relative perturbation. Compounding over 94 layers may still be fatal, but the stated magnitude is ~8x too large.

THE ONE KNOB THE LEDGER LEAVES FREE WAS NEVER TESTED. m was fixed at 4, spending 1.02 percent of headroom. m=64 (the entire survivor set) at scalar granularity costs 8,855,552 bits = 16.4 percent of headroom and is LEGAL. Predictors are also selected by router tally, not by correlation with the target: no greedy/OMP selection over the 64 survivors was run. 'There is no low-dimensional surviving-expert subspace that contains a cold expert's function' is asserted from a single non-optimally-chosen 4-dimensional subspace. Extrapolating the lane's own m=1 -> m=4 gain (L3: sum of squared correlations 0.0133 -> 0.0235) puts m=64 at roughly 0.95 rel error, so the verdict most likely survives - but it is untested where the budget permits.

SELF-CHECK DOES NOT COVER THE CLAIM. 7/7 mutations to the measurement path leave `--demo` green. The bit ledger and the synthetic solver are well covered (8/9); the empirical verdict has no runnable check.

PROBE IS EASIER THAN THE CORPUS AT TWO OF THREE LAYERS. probe_top8_retained 0.773/0.793 vs calibration 0.702/0.738 at L0/L3, i.e. the probe sentence routes MORE into survivors than the calibration corpus, biasing measured damage down at exactly the layers chosen for being worst. Not disclosed.

INHERITED BOUND ABUSE IN THE PREMISE. Lane E makes no Shannon claim of its own (clean), but it imports qwen_structural_plan, whose rd_floor() = sqrt(2^-2R) is the memoryless-Gaussian D(R) - an UPPER bound - still named and documented as a 'rate-distortion floor' and still emitted as gate_up_rd_floor_rel_error in every arm, after commit 356bf9ef corrected exactly this error elsewhere. The S64-vs-S32 case Lane E is adjudicating rests on that wording.

TIMING NARRATIVE INCONSISTENCY (observational). L0 completes in 1.7s, which implies on the order of 1 GB/s of expert-shard reads, while the depth_caveat justifies abandoning L46/L70 by a disk saturated at ~500 MB/s. Warm page cache on early shards explains it, but the report leans on saturation as the reason it has no mid-depth data.

blind_to: delete the survivor -inf mask in probe_layer
collapse the held-out score rows onto the fit rows
drop the attention residual in expert_inputs
replace SwiGLU with a bare gate matmul
self_check.conclusion: demo() covers the bit ledger and a synthetic least-squares solver. Every number the verdict rests on is produced by code the self-check never executes.
